subject: Mathematics
title: Imaginary Numbers
description: A short lesson to help you apply Imaginary Numbers.

hook.text: What times itself gives negative one? No real number works, so mathematicians invented one.

buildup.text: They called it i for imaginary. Descartes meant it as an insult, but the name stuck.

discovery.text: Imaginary numbers turned out essential for solving equations real numbers can't handle.

twist.text: Engineers use them daily. AC circuits, quantum physics, and signal processing all need i.

climax.text: Combine real and imaginary numbers and you get the complex plane—a map for all of algebra.

punchline.text: The most useful number in engineering is imaginary.

quiz.question: What is the imaginary unit i defined as?
